data.title: Comex Copper Ends the Week 7.20% Higher at $5.0580 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month Comex Copper for October delivery gained 33.95 cents per pound, or 7.20% to $5.0580 this week

  • Largest one week net and percentage gain since the week ending July 11, 2025
  • Up for two consecutive weeks
  • Up 48.90 cents or 10.70% over the last two weeks
  • Largest two week gain since the week ending July 11, 2025
  • Largest two week percentage gain since the week ending Nov. 11, 2022
  • Up three of the past four weeks
  • Today it is up 16.00 cents or 3.27%
  • Largest one day dollar and percentage gain since Wednesday, Sept. 24, 2025
  • Up for three consecutive sessions
  • Up 25.30 cents or 5.27% over the last three sessions
  • Largest three day dollar and percentage gain since Thursday, July 10, 2025
  • Up nine of the past 11 sessions
  • Highest settlement value since Wednesday, July 30, 2025
  • Off 12.72% from its 52-week high of $5.795 hit Wednesday, July 23, 2025
  • Up 26.89% from its 52-week low of $3.986 hit Tuesday, Dec. 31, 2024
  • Rose 11.84% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 12.72% from its 2025 settlement high of $5.795 hit Wednesday, July 23, 2025
  • Up 26.81% from its 2025 settlement low of $3.9885 hit Thursday, Jan. 2, 2025
  • Off 12.72% from its record high of $5.795 hit Wednesday, July 23, 2025
  • Year-to-date it is up $1.072 or 26.89%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Trump's trade war: Tariff deadlines and key upcoming events
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff strategy has injected uncertainty into global financial markets, exacerbated by legal challenges to the levies and Trump's push for bilateral trade deals.

Trump's order on "reciprocal" tariffs for exports from 69 trading partners listing higher import duty rates of 10% to 41% kicked in on August 7, raising the average U.S. import duty to its highest in a century.

The latest round of levies includes a 100% tariff on branded or patented pharmaceutical imports and a 25% duty on heavy-duty trucks, as Trump intensifies efforts to reshape global trade.

Here is a timeline for upcoming events and dates that could have a bearing on U.S. tariff policy:

October 1

The United States imposed a 100% tariff on imports of branded or patented pharmaceutical products, with some exceptions for companies building a manufacturing plant in the United States. Trump also imposed 25% levies on heavy-duty trucks.

October 6-7

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's office said he plans a trip to Washington to discuss economic and security issues and including an October 7 meeting with Trump.

October 14

Trump's 10% tariffs on imported timber and lumber and 25% duties on kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities and upholstered furniture come into effect.

November 10

The U.S.-China tariff truce is extended through early November, providing a reprieve during the seasonal surge in imports ahead of the year-end holiday season.

January 1, 2026

Duties will rise further on January 1, 2026 to 30% for upholstered furniture and 50% for cabinets and vanities from countries without trade agreements.

data.title: Week's Best: 'Big Bang Theory' Alum's New Digital Venture — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kenneth Corbin

When Kunal Nayyar, an actor best known for playing Raj Koothrappali on The Big Bang Theory, was trying to organize a trip for his parents from India to the U.S., he struggled to keep track of details such as flight numbers and hotel confirmations. The company he co-founded, IQ121, aims to address those challenges with a secure document storage and sharing system. His company is built with families such as his in mind, whose members might live very far apart and could benefit from a shared digital storage mechanism for important paperwork.

Among other most-read wealth management articles this week:

Time to cut off the spigot? More than three-quarters of wealthy parents are helping their children pay for big-ticket items such as a down payment on a house or graduate school, while nearly two-thirds are paying for routine expenses such as phone bills and other living costs, according to a new survey from Ameriprise Financial. All that support may be taking its toll. More than a third of survey respondents say they are concerned that the support they are providing to their children could hamper their retirement plans, a challenge Ameriprise's Deana Healy suggests parents could address by setting clearer boundaries with their children.

Accusations mount in megabreakaway lawsuit . Merrill Lynch and a large advisory team that left to launch an independent practice have been trading accusations in an escalating legal battle turning on allegations of corporate raiding and misappropriation of trade secrets. Merrill had sued, seeking a restraining order against the team, which had been part of a unit managing $129 billion at the brokerage. The group left to go indie with the assistance of Dynasty Financial Partners and custodian Charles Schwab, each of which is named as a defendant in the lawsuit. A judge this week denied Merrill's request for a temporary restraining order.

Vanguard's nudge campaign . Vanguard has been investing heavily to improve its website, apps, and its general technology apparatus. Less noticed enhancements have aimed to move investors toward better decisions, what Vanguard calls "nudges" that aim to address common mistakes such as leaving money sitting in cash after rolling over an account from a 401(k) to an IRA. Vanguard's response is to send out nudging emails reminding clients that their funds aren't invested. The company says it plans to have about a dozen such nudges implemented by the end of the year.

Try this tax play for your muni bonds . Investors who have enjoyed attractive returns on investment-grade municipal bonds in recent years could be in for a harsh surprise when they sell those bonds or they mature or are called. That is because of the de minimis rule that imposes ordinary tax treatment — as opposed to capital gains rates — on deeply discounted municipal bonds that our guest columnist calls a "ticking tax bomb." Investors might be able to avoid that tax hit by swapping bonds that would be subject to the de minimis rule for full-coupon bonds, our columnist suggests.

Where are the fixed-income opportunities these days ? With credit spreads tightening and interest rates dropping, income-focused investors are seeing diminishing yields from traditional instruments such as Treasuries and corporate bonds. So where are advisors seeing opportunities for attractive risk-adjusted yield these days? We put that question to a handful of top advisors in this week's Big Q feature, and heard about the attractive upside of municipal bonds, private real estate debt, and, for those who can tolerate the risk, high-yield bonds.

Write to advisor.editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Navamedic ASA: Mandatory notification of trades by primary insiders and their close associates
data.description:
Navamedic ASA

Oslo, 3 October 2025: Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement published by Navamedic ASA (the "Company") on 22 September 2025, regarding the commencement of the subscription period in the partly underwritten rights issue of up to 6,046,511 new shares in the Company, at a subscription price of NOK 21.50 per share (the "Rights Issue") and the receipt of subscription rights in the Rights Issue by certain primary insiders, and certain close associates of primary insiders, of the Company.

The Company has been informed that certain primary insiders and certain close associates of primary insiders have subscribed for new shares in the Rights Issue:

Soleglad Invest AS, a company closely associated with Kathrine Elisabeth Gamborg Andreassen, CEO of the Company, has subscribed for 23,260 new shares.

Jostein Davidsen, Chairperson of the Board of Directors of the Company, has subscribed for 17,116 new shares.

Philip Slätis, Commercial Director RX & International of the Company, has subscribed for 3,422 new shares.

Tranbergkollen Invest AS, a company closely associated with Astrid T. Bratvedt, Chief Scientific Officer of the Company, has subscribed for 25,000 new shares.

Jurs AS, a company closely associated with Morten Jurs, member of the Board of Directors of the Company, has subscribed for 7,851 new shares.

Please see the attached forms for further details about the transactions.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation and section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

https://news.cision.com/se/navamedic-asa/r/navamedic-asa--mandatory-notification-of-trades-by-primary-insiders-and-their-close-associates%2Cc4245453
